title: Hogcock!

jokes.text: "I'm sorry... What's a girl's bike? Is that like a girl doctor? Go back to Saudi Arabia, Hitler!"
jokes.setup: Innocent question about buying a girl's bike
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to comparing bikes to doctors to calling someone Hitler
jokes.explanation: Sharp escalation from reasonable to absurd, good economy, represents toxic internet culture well

jokes.text: "You're buying a bike but not a helmet? The head is where the child's brain is. Why don't you get educated, double Hitler?"
jokes.setup: Previous Hitler comment established the pattern
jokes.punchline: Double Hitler and redundant brain location explanation
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation with 'double Hitler' and absurd explanation of where brains are located

jokes.text: "Helmets inhibit brain development. You might as well give your darling child vaccines, which studies show cause homosexuality."
jokes.punchline: Anti-vaccine conspiracy combined with homophobia presented as fact
jokes.explanation: Satirizes conspiracy theorist mindset but relies on shock value

jokes.text: "So what? My two-year-old is super gay, and we love him more than a straight child because he doesn't rape!"
jokes.setup: Previous comment about vaccines causing homosexuality
jokes.punchline: Embracing gay child with horrible reasoning about rape
jokes.explanation: Pushes shock value too far, loses comedic value in offensive territory

jokes.text: "We're now providing the same level of service to our subscribers at zero the cost."
jokes.setup: Moving customer service to India with no phones
jokes.punchline: Admitting they provide no service while claiming efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack corporate doublespeak, admitting terrible service while spinning it as positive

jokes.text: "And then we'll be able to get even better trophy wives... Halfasian ones!"
jokes.setup: Talk of company perfection and success
jokes.punchline: Reducing women to racial fetish objects as business reward
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack chauvinism but relies too heavily on offensive stereotypes

jokes.text: "I never told you this, but I once came up late at night looking for Jack, and you were in a wedding dress dancing with one of his suits."
jokes.setup: Jonathan trying to block Liz from Jack's office
jokes.punchline: Revealing absurdly intimate/creepy behavior with Jack's clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jonathan's creepy devotion to Jack, vivid visual image, excellent character work

jokes.text: To keep away Frankensteins, which, as far as we know, worked.
jokes.punchline: Treating monster prevention as legitimate goal with measurable success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery treating absurd premise as business logic

jokes.text: "He has a degree in ethno-musicology from Wesleyan, so he's a receptionist in a dental office."
jokes.setup: Explaining Criss has gone back to work
jokes.punchline: Prestigious liberal arts degree leading to menial job
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure, relatable observation about liberal arts degrees vs job market

jokes.text: I ran this morning for 30 minutes. Does that include dry heaving? And wet.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to both types of vomiting during exercise
jokes.explanation: Good character-based physical comedy with escalation

jokes.text: "Hogcock! Which is a combination of 'hogwash' and 'poppycock.'"
jokes.setup: Liz rejecting Jack's claim about being emotionally fine
jokes.punchline: Making up a nonsense word then immediately explaining it
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz linguistic creativity, great structure with immediate explanation, very quotable

jokes.text: "if my mother told me that everything I had been raised to believe was a lie and then died, I'd be like, 'say what?'"
jokes.setup: Describing traumatic life events Jack experienced
jokes.punchline: Massive underreaction to devastating news
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor, captures Liz's awkward responses to serious situations

jokes.text: "A city built on the religion of capitalism, and I am its high priest, looking down on the swinish multitude."
jokes.setup: Jack showing off his office view
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top religious metaphor for corporate power
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack grandiosity, great character voice with religious capitalism metaphor

jokes.text: "even they must acknowledge me... As a God."
jokes.setup: Talking about people who hate him
jokes.punchline: Ultimate narcissistic conclusion about his divine status
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of Jack's ego, though predictable for the character

jokes.text: "Dark times are these." - Yoda quote delivered seriously
jokes.setup: Jack saying he's reminded of something Yoda said
jokes.punchline: Using Star Wars wisdom for corporate existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but relies on simple pop culture reference without much twist

jokes.text: "But we thought maybe contestants could win money instead of penis punches."
jokes.setup: Adapting Japanese game show for American audiences
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of penis punches as original prize
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between American expectations and absurd Japanese game show elements

jokes.text: "And yet you still say stupid stuff to me all the time and suck at carrying boxes!"
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about Kenneth being president
jokes.punchline: Specific, petty complaints undermining his argument
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic, specific details make the complaint funnier

jokes.text: "Chickpeas, moonshine, turtle meat?"
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering refreshments as network president
jokes.punchline: Bizarre hillbilly menu items instead of corporate offerings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character work, incongruous rural offerings in corporate setting

jokes.text: "You've always had the body for it."
jokes.setup: Liz saying she's concentrating on being a mom
jokes.punchline: Kenneth thinking she means physical requirements for motherhood
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth innocent misunderstanding, slightly awkward but true to character

jokes.text: "Woman, writer, New York... Those are all on my list of TV no-no words."
jokes.setup: Liz pitching show about woman writer in New York
jokes.punchline: Network executive rejecting based on demographic buzzword bingo
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry satire, great economy, perfectly captures network executive mindset

jokes.text: "They don't want to watch some angry New York crankypants make that face... Exactly."
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing what audiences don't want
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately making the exact face he's describing
jokes.explanation: Great visual callback to Kenneth's description, perfect timing and character work

jokes.text: "Shows where a guy gets a drink thrown in his face, and then he turns to his dog and says, 'don't even say it.' To his dog!"
jokes.punchline: Absurd scenario of talking to dog with human-like awareness
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor, captures lowest-common-denominator TV tropes effectively

jokes.text: "I'll go to cable, where you can swear and really take time to let moments land."
jokes.punchline: Cable TV pretensions delivered in rapid-fire network pace
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about cable vs network differences, good irony in delivery

jokes.text: "I will tell the other gays your real ages!"
jokes.punchline: Age as ultimate weapon among gay community
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character work, plays on stereotype but with specificity

jokes.text: "This is an actor announcement. The show's over, so you're not technically an actor anymore."
jokes.punchline: Brutal practical reality delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jenna's ego with bureaucratic precision

jokes.text: "I will stop being an actress when the Earth stops spinning on Kabbalah monster's fingernail."
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical metaphor mixing cosmology and Kabbalah
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna nonsense logic, creative absurd imagery mixing mysticism and drama

jokes.text: "Good-bye forever, you factory reject dildos."
jokes.punchline: Specific, bizarre insult combining industrial and sexual metaphors
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with unexpected specificity, very Jenna voice

jokes.text: "I'm afraid I'm gonna have to spank you again."
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of workplace spanking as repeat offense
jokes.explanation: Jack's casual inappropriate behavior, but relies on shock more than wit

jokes.text: "Where are all the baby pigeons?"
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying he's been thinking about something
jokes.punchline: Completely unexpected urban wildlife observation
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth non-sequitur, actually relatable observation delivered unexpectedly

jokes.text: "It's like when a pig says, 'if I can communicate with you telepathically, do I have a soul?' And then you're like, 'no. Duh. Into the slaughtering chute.'"
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of philosophical pig's existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth absurdism, mixing philosophy with rural practicality, incredibly original

jokes.text: "I'm going to crush this problem... With my ass."
jokes.punchline: Taking acronym literally with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Good structure and Jack delivery, though pun setup is a bit obvious

jokes.text: "That's Tracy... 'T' as in the drink, 'R' as in the pirate noise, 'A' as in the Fonzie noise, 'C' as in sea monster, 'Y' as in why do we even make friends if they're gonna let you down when we need them the most?"
jokes.setup: Tracy spelling his name for Kenneth's assistant
jokes.punchline: Phonetic alphabet deteriorating into emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure, perfect Tracy voice, brilliant emotional breakdown through mundane task

jokes.text: "'J' as in the birds I'm afraid of..."
jokes.punchline: Unexplained fear of birds starting with J
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but less impactful than the main Tracy spelling bit

jokes.text: "For example, the rapper T.I., who wrote, 'better get on yo job, tell 'em. Haters get on yo job, nougats.' At least, I think he was saying 'nougats.'"
jokes.setup: Quoting rapper to support work ethic argument
jokes.punchline: Mishearing profanity as candy reference
jokes.explanation: Decent culture clash humor, though setup is a bit forced

jokes.text: "I'll be the one wearing a purple sweater and wrapping a baby swing around some skank's neck."
jokes.setup: Arranging to meet for a fight
jokes.punchline: Specific, disturbing murder method using playground equipment
jokes.explanation: Too dark and specific, crosses into uncomfortable territory without enough comedic payoff

jokes.text: "The parking space closest to the door is mine."
jokes.punchline: Ultimate achievement is premium parking spot
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Jack's priorities, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: "And if you can, get hit by a car. You get to spend a couple days in the hospital, and they'll give you soup."
jokes.setup: Weather report advice about bundling up
jokes.punchline: Recommending intentional injury for free healthcare
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about healthcare system, good contrast with weather report format

jokes.text: "What you're proposing is a sin, Jack, but she is wicked hot."
jokes.punchline: Religious objection immediately overruled by attraction
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing moral flexibility, economical delivery

jokes.text: "It would have to be a conjugal visit, because I am in a Puerto Rican jail."
jokes.punchline: Practical constraint due to incarceration
jokes.explanation: Good callback to character history, matter-of-fact delivery enhances absurdity

jokes.text: "My foot hurts, but I'm okay."
jokes.setup: Jenna playing dead body on Law & Order
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to complain about minor discomfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna priority inversion, excellent character work showing her self-centeredness

jokes.text: "I think this injury has given me crime-solving powers. It's Mind beauty."
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical explanation of psychic detective abilities
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna logic, creative nonsense phrase 'Mind beauty'

jokes.text: "Good-bye forever, you soup line at a gay homeless shelter."
jokes.punchline: Overly specific, bizarre comparison for farewell insult
jokes.explanation: Creative but convoluted insult, very Jenna voice but less elegant than earlier examples

jokes.text: Crisstopher Rick Chros. Are you kidding me?
jokes.punchline: Realizing the unfortunate acronym implications
jokes.explanation: Mild name-based humor with obvious setup

jokes.text: "My God! Since when do you listen to T.I.?"
jokes.setup: Confronting the person from message board
jokes.punchline: Amazement that Criss knows hip-hop references
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but fairly straightforward surprise

jokes.text: "Now I'm all jittery and weird and... bird!"
jokes.setup: Criss describing too much coffee at work
jokes.punchline: Distracted by random bird mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Criss's childlike attention span, economical delivery

jokes.text: "Or that one where your hands are spiders. Hand spiders."
jokes.setup: Criss describing waiting room games for kids
jokes.punchline: Redundant clarification of obvious concept
jokes.explanation: Good Criss character moment showing his childlike enthusiasm

jokes.text: "You're... the dad."
jokes.setup: Discussion of who should work vs stay home
jokes.punchline: Assigning traditional gender roles based on actual preferences
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy, smart role reversal based on personalities

jokes.text: "like, I don't know, a show about a dentist's office where the sassy hygienist says things like, 'I'm turning 30... Again.'"
jokes.setup: Criss suggesting show ideas for Liz
jokes.punchline: Generic sitcom premise with obvious catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good satire of generic sitcom formulas, captures bad TV writing well

jokes.text: "Damn it! Why did I get such a loud pacemaker?"
jokes.setup: Tracy hiding and trying to be quiet
jokes.punchline: Unexpected medical device causing noise problems
jokes.explanation: Good random Tracy problem, unexpected medical device issue

jokes.text: "I saw his schedule on his assistant's pornography box!"
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining how he knows Kenneth's schedule
jokes.punchline: Calling computer a 'pornography box'
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy technology misunderstanding, though fairly predictable for the character

jokes.text: "Unless you need us to hate the new Taylor Swift album. That girl has feelings. You're wrong, dotcom!"
jokes.setup: Dotcom saying they can help Tracy with anything
jokes.punchline: Tracy's strong defense of Taylor Swift's emotional authenticity
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Tracy's unexpected music taste

jokes.text: "Neither is talking two catholic beauties into a delicious vanilla-caramel sex swirl, but I did it."
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying upward spirals aren't a thing
jokes.punchline: Jack's elaborate metaphor for threesome
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack sexual bragging but metaphor is overwrought

jokes.text: "And I got rid of their accents."
jokes.setup: Jack talking about his sexual conquest
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of accent elimination as achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack control obsession, great economy and character specificity

jokes.text: "Jack, porking in that prison basement was wicked awesome."
jokes.punchline: Crude description with lost accent proving Jack's claim
jokes.explanation: Good callback proving Jack's claim but relies on crude shock value

jokes.text: "You string cheese with a tooth stuck in it."
jokes.punchline: Specific, bizarre food-based insult
jokes.explanation: Creative Jack insult with vivid, disgusting imagery

jokes.text: "They burned you in effigy! The hair went up like a Roman candle."
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic detail about effigy burning
jokes.explanation: Good visual detail and Jonathan's excited delivery

jokes.text: "God, I feel like I have a 'macropenis' right now."
jokes.setup: Jack feeling powerful from negative attention
jokes.punchline: Made-up word for enhanced masculinity feeling
jokes.explanation: Good Jack power metaphor, though somewhat predictable sexual reference

jokes.text: "He loves his family, but he hates the rat race. He's 'hardly working.'"
jokes.punchline: Obvious pun on 'hard working' with character name
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's too obvious and predictable for 30 Rock standards

jokes.text: "If fewer than 150 episodes of TGS are produced in total, Mr. Jordan shall be paid a penalty of $30 million."
jokes.punchline: Absurd penalty amount for not making enough episodes
jokes.explanation: Good plot setup, captures absurdity of TV contracts

jokes.text: One last chance for TGS to make America Say, 'what? Why?'
jokes.punchline: Show's goal is confused viewer response
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on show's confusing nature

jokes.text: "Shut your chin slit."
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific anatomical insult
jokes.explanation: Creative Liz insult with great specificity and economy

jokes.text: "You Eastern European knockoff Mr. Potato heads."
jokes.punchline: Elaborate toy-based ethnic insult
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna insult style but not as sharp as her best work

jokes.text: Shut it down. I will return to my first love... Broadway!
jokes.setup: Jenna at airport deciding on next move
jokes.punchline: Sudden dramatic career pivot announcement
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna dramatic moment with sudden decision

jokes.text: "I know what it is to blow up overnight. And I'm not talking about my gout."
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he means career success, not medical swelling
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke, good Tracy medical reference

jokes.text: "So, like the snakes I kept in my dressing room, I release you."
jokes.setup: Tracy releasing Kenneth from his promise
jokes.punchline: Comparing to literal snake release
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy metaphor with perfect character detail about dressing room snakes

jokes.text: "And while we're naming things, my car ran out of gas on the Long Island expressway."
jokes.punchline: Random practical problem interrupting sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment undermining emotional beats with practical concerns

jokes.text: I'm looking for six figures, eight if you're counting cents, which I fell for once before... Not cool, the gap.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to being tricked by retail pricing
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor about retail confusion

jokes.text: "I pissed off my enemies... Pelosi, Maddow, Baldwin."
jokes.punchline: Including Alec Baldwin (the actor) as personal enemy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke with Alec Baldwin playing character who hates Alec Baldwin

jokes.text: "You're just an alcoholic with a great voice."
jokes.setup: Liz angry at Jack for life advice
jokes.punchline: Reducing Jack to his drinking problem and vocal talent
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight disguised as insult, excellent economy

jokes.text: "eating night cheese and transitioning my pajamas into daywear."
jokes.punchline: Specific details of slobbish behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character work, 'night cheese' is iconic, great specificity

jokes.text: "I used to be a shark, and then you 'unsharkulated' me."
jokes.setup: Jack blaming Liz for changing him
jokes.punchline: Made-up word for removing shark-like qualities
jokes.explanation: Good Jack wordplay, creative neologism fits character voice

jokes.text: I called you up here for one meeting seven years ago, and you kept coming up.
jokes.punchline: Reducing entire series to Liz's persistent visits
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on the entire series premise

jokes.text: There! You're no longer special to him. Get out! Get out of our lives! Yes!
jokes.setup: Kenneth observing Jack and Liz's breakup
jokes.punchline: Celebrating elimination of romantic rival
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth possessiveness with emotional victory

jokes.text: "NBC... the same company that suggested we get flush buddies to save water."
jokes.punchline: Absurd cost-cutting measure involving bathroom sharing
jokes.explanation: Good corporate absurdity, 'flush buddies' is memorably disgusting

jokes.text: "My God, this will change elder care forever. Whoops. Nope."
jokes.setup: Liz's optimism about the hugging machine
jokes.punchline: Immediate failure of the invention
jokes.explanation: Great deflation structure, perfect economy in the failure

jokes.text: "The only thing that can stop this show is an act of God or if some genius figured out a way to stall just long enough..."
jokes.punchline: Setting up his own sabotage plan
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Tracy's schemes, effective foreshadowing

jokes.text: "The night is young. And neither are you."
jokes.setup: Tracy threatening to sabotage the show
jokes.punchline: Age-based insult with grammar twist
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with good economy, though not especially memorable

jokes.text: No. / Blimpie's. / No! / Come on, Lutz!
jokes.setup: Lutz finally getting to pick lunch
jokes.punchline: Universal horror at his terrible choice
jokes.explanation: Good payoff of Lutz's terrible taste with group reaction

jokes.text: "You picked last time, Liz, so alphabetically after Lemon comes..."
jokes.punchline: Building to obvious Lutz selection
jokes.explanation: Good setup structure building suspense to inevitable Lutz disappointment

jokes.text: I found this customer-loyalty card to a place called blazer bar, and I assume it's yours. / Thank you. It's Manhattan's largest out-of-business women's blazer dump.
jokes.punchline: Store's pathetically specific business model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character detail with absurd business concept

jokes.text: "Look, I didn't like the way we ended things yesterday."
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz had major fight
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of their brutal argument
jokes.explanation: Good understatement but fairly predictable for the situation

jokes.text: "most of Tan Penis Island was destroyed in Sting's house fire."
jokes.setup: Jack correcting Liz's assumption about his travels
jokes.punchline: Bizarre fictional island with celebrity disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd imagery, 'Tan Penis Island' is memorable, good celebrity reference

jokes.text: "'snowicane' white lady name like dorva or something."
jokes.punchline: Racial observation about storm naming conventions
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor but treads carefully around sensitive territory

jokes.text: "So get up on that stage and cut the B.S.!"
jokes.punchline: Censoring herself with abbreviation
jokes.explanation: Weak joke, predictable Liz self-censoring

jokes.text: "Did you pin her up against the wall? Were your shirts wet with rain?"
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about Jack and Liz's argument
jokes.punchline: Turning argument into romance novel cliché
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna romanticizing everything, good romance novel parody

jokes.text: Did you pin her up against the wall? Were your shirts wet with rain?
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about Jack and Liz's argument
jokes.punchline: Expecting rom-com cliches in their fight
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna romantic fantasy applied to platonic relationship

jokes.text: "She did want me to cancel Top Chef because Colicchio's lunch place changed the toppings on her favorite salad."
jokes.punchline: Absurdly petty reason for professional vendetta
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz pettiness, great specificity with real celebrity chef reference

jokes.text: "We were gonna lose our virginity to each other! Now I'll never lose it!"
jokes.setup: Conan confronting Liz about their past
jokes.punchline: Blaming her for his permanent virginity
jokes.explanation: Good pathetic humor, works as example of Liz's grudge-holding

jokes.text: "I frenched her dad on new year's."
jokes.setup: Jenna worried about telling Liz something
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of making out with Liz's father
jokes.explanation: Good shock value with perfect Jenna casualness about inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: "I spent Christmas alone in the Hamptons, drinking scotch and throwing firecrackers at Billy Joel's dog!"
jokes.punchline: Specific, petty celebrity harassment while drunk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant combination of Jack's privilege and pettiness, very specific and quotable

jokes.text: "Daddy, please stop crying."
jokes.punchline: Jenna's inappropriate paternal response
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna response showing her weird relationship dynamic with Jack

jokes.text: "I'm five steps ahead of you, you sons of bitches!"
jokes.punchline: Uncharacteristic competence and aggression
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lutz character reversal, rare moment of triumph

jokes.text: "Okay, I can't do this anymore. I've never met Mickey Rourke."
jokes.punchline: Admitting she makes up celebrity stories
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Jenna's deceptions

jokes.text: On behalf of the network, Ms. Maroney, I think you should sing at the end of the show tonight. / Wonderful! I'll need ten pianos.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering Jenna a performance slot
jokes.punchline: Jenna's immediate over-the-top requirements
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna diva behavior with ridiculous rider demands

jokes.text: "Good-bye, long-hair guy. Good-bye, Richard Esposito. Go home to your wife and eight beautiful children."
jokes.punchline: Overly specific details about random crew member
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy sentimentality with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Guys, I know I don't normally say much, but... / What, woman? Speak!
jokes.punchline: Frank's impatient demand after acknowledging her silence
jokes.explanation: Good background character moment with Frank's typical rudeness

jokes.text: "You changed your name to 'Aardvark'? That's insane!"
jokes.punchline: Someone else's more extreme alphabetical manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation beyond Lutz's scheme, classic writer room competition

jokes.text: "To act drunk, you just wear two different-sized heels."
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining acting is cheap tricks
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd method acting technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific acting advice, perfect Jenna logic, very quotable

jokes.text: "And to cry, you just clutch a shard of broken glass."
jokes.punchline: Dangerous method for achieving emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to heel joke, escalates to concerning territory

jokes.text: "Just bury the necklace and move on."
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude toward his own death and replacement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth dark wisdom, matter-of-fact about mortality

jokes.text: "You know what a real man does? Fakes his own death."
jokes.punchline: Tracy's twisted idea of masculinity
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic inversion, dark humor with character specificity

jokes.text: "Why would a guy planning to fake his death deposit $70? He wouldn't!"
jokes.punchline: Reverse psychology with oddly specific amount
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy conspiracy thinking with specific detail

jokes.text: "Grizz just found out his uncle left him a bed-and-breakfast outside Santa Fe. I'm gonna be a real fish out of water."
jokes.punchline: Large urban man inheriting quaint rural business
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast, gentle humor about lifestyle change

jokes.text: "Because for seven years, you have yelled at me and turned the lights out on me when I was in the bathroom and written on me while I was sleeping"
jokes.punchline: Specific catalog of workplace abuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of abuse, perfect Lutz victimization catalog

jokes.text: "And when that sandwich slides out of you in a week, look at it, because that is Lutz's revenge!"
jokes.punchline: Disturbing digestive imagery as ultimate revenge
jokes.explanation: Good Lutz moment but relies too heavily on gross-out humor

jokes.text: "Give it up for Liz Lemon, everybody! The least molested person in here."
jokes.setup: MC introducing Liz at strip club
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about sexual assault
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that crosses into uncomfortable territory without enough comedic payoff

jokes.text: "Anybody who's ever left me in my life just left... My dad going to get a pack of smokes and never coming back"
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he won't say goodbye
jokes.punchline: Classic abandonment trope delivered with genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Good emotional truth from Tracy, though relies on familiar trope

jokes.text: "But because the human heart is not properly connected to the human brain, I love you, and I'm gonna miss you."
jokes.setup: Liz giving honest goodbye to Tracy
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific explanation for emotional contradiction
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Liz line combining humor with genuine emotion, very quotable

jokes.text: "Brian Williams needs a mirror on the floor of his bathroom. I guess you want that if you have a glass toilet."
jokes.punchline: Logical explanation for bizarre celebrity request
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth logic connecting absurd celebrity lifestyle details

jokes.text: "Cupcake sandwich! Cupcake sandwich!"
jokes.punchline: Childlike excitement over absurd food combination
jokes.explanation: Good Liz food moment, captures her regression around quality food

jokes.text: "This is like what happened with my gynecologist all over again!"
jokes.setup: Liz blaming herself for Jack's apparent suicide
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous tragic misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good callback suggesting pattern of Liz-related tragedies

jokes.text: "Go to YouTube and search 'Hamlet the mini pig goes downstairs.'"
jokes.setup: Jack leaving final gift for Liz
jokes.punchline: Specific viral video recommendation as parting gesture
jokes.explanation: Good absurd final gesture, specific enough to be memorable

jokes.text: "Don goes to work for Peggy!"
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Mad Men ending to save Jack
jokes.punchline: Jack casually spoiling major plot point
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture moment but relies on external reference

jokes.text: "It's a word that comes to us by way of the old high German luba, from the Latin lubere, meaning 'to be pleasing.'"
jokes.setup: Jack avoiding saying he loves Liz
jokes.punchline: Over-elaborate etymology to avoid emotional directness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack intellectual avoidance of emotion, great character work

jokes.text: "A hot bowl of bear meat or your enemy's skull... Split."
jokes.punchline: Violent historical examples of affection
jokes.explanation: Good Jack historical knowledge applied to emotional situation

jokes.text: "Clear dishwashers, so you can see what's going on inside it."
jokes.setup: Jack having breakthrough moment on boat
jokes.punchline: Absurdly simple product innovation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack business mind applied to mundane appliance problem

jokes.text: "I mean, who's Pete? I have amnesia! I... Damn it! I practiced this!"
jokes.punchline: Breaking character and admitting he rehearsed
jokes.explanation: Good Pete incompetence moment, classic amateur acting failure

jokes.text: "Wait. You're Sam?" "Don't even say it."
jokes.punchline: Immediate confusion about Sam's identity
jokes.explanation: Decent identity confusion gag but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: "See ya later, suckers! Meep-meep! Pyung!"
jokes.punchline: Road Runner sound effects plus random noise
jokes.explanation: Decent Jenna moment but relies on simple cartoon reference

jokes.text: "My dad finally came back from getting cigarettes."
jokes.punchline: Impossible resolution to childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier emotional moment with absurd resolution

tmdb_overview: Liz has trouble adjusting to being a stay-at-home mother. Jack takes stock of his life, and Tracy struggles to get on without Kenneth.
insight: Final season delivers 120 jokes in 42 minutes—a career-high density that sustains the absurdist pace.
